{{Quote|We are the Elders, descendants of the Rakata priests who served during the days of the Infinite Empire over 20,000 of your galactic standard years ago.|An Elder councillor, to [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
The '''Elders,''' also known as the '''Red Rakata,''' were a tribe of [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]]. Their ancestry was the Priest Caste of the Rakata of old during the latter [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]].

==History==
{{Quote|We are the keepers of knowledge and the guardians of the Temple of the Ancients. During the collapse of the Rakata Empire, the warlords unleashed weapons of terrible destruction that threatened to drive our species to extinction. In the devastation's aftermath our ancestors locked the knowledge of such technology away in the Temple so it could never be used again. Ever since then, we Elders have kept our vigil.|An Elder councillor, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
On [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]], Rakatan civilization rapidly deteriorated. Warlords devastated the surface of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with weapons of mass destruction. The majority of the Rakata were forced to flee underground. Protected by their ancient temples and enclaves, only the Elders remained on the planet's surface.<ref name="KOTOR" />

[[File:Thredrakata.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Rakatan Elder]]
During the [[Jedi Civil War]], 20,000 years after the fall of the Infinite Empire, the elders worked to use the ancient technology to restore order and help the survival of the ancient knowledge and traditions of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]]. They were constantly at war with other violent Rakata, such as the [[Black Rakata]], and feared their [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] beasts.<ref name="KOTOR" />

The Elders desired to destroy the [[Star Forge]] so that they could finally put their bloody past behind them, but there was a problem. They could no longer enter their own [[Temple of the Ancients]], the nexus of their collected knowledge and the key to accessing the Star Forge, because [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] was lost to them.<ref name="KOTOR" />

[[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak's]] arrival in the system gave the Elders a perfect opportunity. Promising to destroy the Star Forge, Revan entered the temple, but he betrayed the Elder's trust and took the space station for himself.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Yet Revan would return three years later and redress that betrayal. Amnesiac, he was brought before [[The One]], the leader of the Black Rakata, who again sought his help, but when he finally found the abode of the Elders, they told him about the atrocities of the One. Therefore he did not slaughter them and seeing finally what was 'good', he sided with the Elders; this angered the One who attacked Revan but he, as well as the rest of the Black Rakata, were all killed.<ref name="KOTOR" />

The Elder priests disabled the shields of the [[Temple of the Ancients]], giving Revan access to its secrets.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Revan aided the Elders' efforts to regain their lost [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force sensitivity]] which would eventually allow them to rejoin the galactic community.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Known Elders==
*[[Ll'awa]]<ref name="KOTOR" />
*[[Orsaa]]<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
It is possible for the player to stay loyal to the One and kill the Elders, resulting in the Black Rakata disabling the shields of the Temple. Even after that choice, it is also possible to kill the Black Rakata afterward.
Another possibility is to side with the Elders, but then return to them after falling to the dark side. Telling the Elders that you have returned to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] will make the Elders turn on you, and you can then kill them for dark side points.
